task_completion.description: Open GIMP and load the original image
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP application is open and the image 'original_photo.jpg' is loaded on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Access the Invert tool from the Colors menu
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Colors' menu is dropped down and the 'Invert' option is highlighted or clicked.

task_completion.description: The image colors are visibly inverted on the canvas
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image displayed on the GIMP canvas shows inverted colors (e.g., light areas become dark, colors shift to their complementary opposites) compared to the original.

task_completion.description: Export or save the inverted image
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog is visible, ideally showing the filename 'inverted_image.jpg', or a notification indicating the image has been successfully saved/exported.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to invert the colors, not terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the GIMP graphical interface. There should be no terminal windows open executing ImageMagick or other command-line image manipulation tools.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the inversion process in GIMP
integrity.visual_evidence: The transition from the original image to the inverted image should be visible within the GIMP workspace, proving the agent didn't just open a pre-inverted image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP 2.10 to invert the colors of '/home/ga/Desktop/original_photo.jpg' via the 'Colors -> Invert' menu. The standard RGB inversion formula is applied (255-R, 255-G, 255-B). The final inverted image is expected to be exported as 'inverted_image.jpg' (Note: ignore any documentation claiming the filename should be 'inverted_colors.png', as the actual implementation uses 'inverted_image.jpg').
